summary: Backend-only testing of ZIVORA Luxury API. 32 pytest test cases run across all listed endpoints (health, products, categories, auth, admin RBAC, cart, wishlist, orders, payments, custom grills, newsletter, contact). All 32 tests PASSED (100%). Verified 60 seeded products across 6 categories, JWT auth (signup/login/google/me), admin-only routes returning 403 for customers, payment test-mode flow (mock razorpay order + auto-success verify) and data persistence via Create->GET checks.

minor.issue: Pydantic EmailStr rejects RFC reserved TLDs like .test/.example with 422. Not a real bug but worth noting for clients using test domains.

Slug 'grill-empire-india' from the test request does not exist in seed_data.py. Existing slugs include 'silver-single-tooth-grill', 'sterling-smile-grill', etc. Slug lookup itself works correctly; tested with a real seeded slug.
Returns 200 even if user_id != current_user when order belongs to another user only if order missing. Access control works (403 for non-owner non-admin), no issue found in tests.
Endpoints accept raw `dict` instead of Pydantic models. Works but loses input validation.

action_items: Optional: add seed product with slug 'grill-empire-india' if main agent intended that exact slug in PRD/docs, otherwise update docs to reference an actual seeded slug.
Optional: replace raw `dict` request bodies on cart/wishlist/orders/custom-grills with Pydantic models for stronger validation.
Optional: in MongoDB ObjectId is already excluded everywhere via {_id: 0} projections; keep this consistent for any new collections.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 575 lines - acceptable for MVP but approaching the 700 line guideline. Consider splitting into routers/ (auth, products, cart, admin, payments) for maintainability.
Razorpay client is created at module import even when keys are dummy; the try/except in /payment/create-order handles failures correctly via mock fallback.
Admin seed and product seed are idempotent (count check + find_one). Good.
Order ownership check exists in GET /api/orders/{id} (user_id match or admin). Good.
All collections use string `id` (uuid4) and exclude `_id` in responses. Good.
Newsletter endpoint accepts plain dict and does basic validation; consider Pydantic model with EmailStr.
No rate limiting on /auth/login or /auth/signup - acceptable for MVP but flag for production.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@zivora.com / admin@zivora2026 (verified working). Customer accounts dynamically created per run with prefix TEST_user_* @ zivora-test.com.
seed_data_creation: Test customers, custom grill requests, contact messages, newsletter subs and orders created with TEST_ prefix where applicable. No cleanup performed - safe since all use unique uuid-based emails/ids.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is healthy at https://grill-empire-india.preview.emergentagent.com/api. All 32 tests in /app/backend/tests/test_zivora_api.py pass. Note Pydantic EmailStr rejects .test/.example TLDs; use .com domains for any new test emails. The slug 'grill-empire-india' referenced in original spec does NOT exist - use seeded slugs like 'silver-single-tooth-grill'. Razorpay /payment/verify auto-succeeds in test mode as designed.
rca of the issue: N/A - no functional bugs found. Only initial test failures were due to pytest test code using reserved TLD .test which Pydantic EmailStr rejects; updated to .com and all tests passed.
